Robin led technical/functional requirements, provided detailed feedback on cost/evaluation/reporting gaps (12 Dec), and drove the demo agenda (9 Jan). Both demonstrated knowledge of the buying process and access to Tanya Roach (Decision Maker) and the finance team, but neither escalated to Tanya or Kelly Thompson (Executive Sponsor) during the evaluation window. competition.narrative: The competitive field is well-documented and Federated's shortlist has narrowed over time, with iValua as the dominant incumbent and peer threat. Competitors named in Salesforce: Coupa, GEP Worldwide, iValua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba (Competitors__c field). iValua is the incumbent, deployed for eSourcing, contract repository, and supplier information management since before Nov 2025 (Tanya Roach, 21 Nov: 'We do have iValua installed... sourcing and contract repository... supplier information management module'). Jessica Lee stated on 21 Nov that Federated is evaluating iValua P2P but 'due to some of the costs associated with it... we're just doing our due diligence' and will 'go to market' to see if 'it makes sense to have two different technologies or would we then potentially change out our sourcing tool.' Robin McDonald confirmed on 12 Dec that 'iValua... they have an insanely slick user interface, they're also a single code base' but 'the gap is frankly cost,' so Federated is considering a unified suite replacement or keeping iValua sourcing and adding a separate P2P module. The other named competitors (Coupa, GEP, JAGGAER, Ariba) were likely part of the vendor discovery process (Nov-Jan 2026) but are not mentioned on any call, so their current status is unknown (likely evaluated and faded or shortlisted but not discussed with Zycus). The decision framing per Jessica (21 Nov) was: stay with iValua for full suite vs. adopt a new unified platform (Zycus or peer) that is more cost-effective. By 9 Jan Kevin Cipollaro (Zycus) acknowledged the iValua migration cost (~$500K implementation for existing eSourcing customers) and offered pricing relief ('if this is gonna be a true partnership, then we'll find pricing alleviation') to overcome the switching cost. The wedge is cost: Zycus must deliver the same or better functionality (evaluation workflows, reporting, user experience) at a materially lower total cost (license + implementation) than iValua P2P + potential sourcing replacement. The threat is iValua's incumbent position, user familiarity, and the switching cost; Zycus's wedge is cost competitiveness, unified code base, AI innovation (Merlin), and implementation flexibility. No other competitor is currently a live peer threat in the calls; the real competition is iValua and the do-nothing/status-quo (which won in Jun 2026 when the project was shelved). How we win: re-engage in spring 2027, build the quantified ROI model showing cost savings vs. iValua, demonstrate evaluation workflow and reporting superiority on the demo follow-up, and secure Tanya/Kelly sponsorship to overcome the status-quo bias.

identify_pain.narrative: Federated's pain is operational inefficiency, lack of control, cost, and adoption risk from poor user experience. Tanya Roach (Director Procurement) described the transformation journey on 21 Nov 2025: '5 years ago we did not have a procurement team... we now have a mature procurement organization... we're doing lots of sourcing, saving lots of money... we do think there's a need now for p2p in the organization' to gain 'more control points' and efficiency from cataloging and guided buying. She stated 'we have no p2p for operational procurement in the organization outside of the refinery,' so manual processes dominate. Robin McDonald (COE Manager) stated on 12 Dec 2025 the core pain is 'the imbalance between cost and quality, with cost being the significant gap' when evaluating iValua P2P, and 'if it's not easy, they're not gonna use the system... procurement practitioners are gonna flow like water... take the least path of resistance.' She noted 'today some of the challenges we have is we're trying to get a data lake created from multiple sources' and current evaluation workflows are weak. Jessica Lee noted on 21 Nov that the lack of P2P means no purchase-order visibility or control across the organization. Sean Jullion's retail-support team runs ~100+ sourcing events per year with 3-4 people (9 Jan), high-volume/low-complexity, and needs speed. Pain owners: Tanya (strategic, organizational maturity and control), Robin (technical/functional, user adoption and cost), Jessica (sourcing/contracting, vendor evaluation and cost), Sean (retail operations, speed and volume). All pain is operational/financial (lack of control, manual work, cost, adoption risk), confirmed across all three calls, no inferred pain.

Robin acknowledged 'there would have to be a lot of work done probably just on our side around the financials and how that would integrate' for future co-op rollout (12 Dec). Paper process is partially mapped (NDA in flight, JDE integration confirmed, legal/procurement involved, partner-led implementation expected, migration cost flagged) but MSA/SOW/InfoSec/contracting timeline are unknown because the buyer shelved the project before those conversations. What would create visibility: re-engage in spring 2027, advance to commercial stage, and formalize the paper process with Jessica and legal.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process has been outlined but not formalized, and the buyer paused it before executing. Jessica Lee stated on 21 Nov 2025 the plan was to 'go to market around February' 2026 for a P2P RFP, evaluating whether to expand iValua or adopt a unified suite. By 12 Dec 2025 Robin McDonald confirmed 'we're trying to get a data lake created... a small vendor shortlist... aiming for an RFI/RFP in Q2 (possibly Q3)' and noted they need to 'get finance team buy-in before issuing formal scope' (9 Jan 2026). The Next Step history shows the team was 'working with Jordan... on registering for RFQ in their Ivalua portal, document expected to be released in April' (pre-9 Jan entry), but the RFQ never materialized. On 2 Jun 2026 Jessica advised 'the initiative has been shelved for the time being and to check in next spring as project has been deprioritized.' The process architecture was: vendor discovery calls (Nov-Jan) → shortlist → RFI/RFP (planned Feb-Apr, then Q2-Q3) → finance buy-in → selection. The call sequence shows Jessica (Senior Manager Corporate Services Procurement) leading vendor vetting, Robin (COE Manager) leading technical requirements, and Tanya (Director) and Kelly (AVP Finance, Executive Sponsor) expected to authorize the RFP and budget but never engaged. The process stalled at the finance-buy-in gate, before the RFP was issued. The approval chain (Tanya → Kelly → finance) was never activated. Re-engaging Jessica to confirm the spring 2027 timing and escalating to Tanya/Kelly to restart the process are the required moves.

decision_criteria.narrative: Federated's evaluation criteria are well-documented across the three calls and center on cost, ease of use, evaluation workflow depth, and reporting. Robin McDonald stated on 12 Dec 2025: 'I think it's gonna come down to the right mix of cost and quality... the gap that we're seeing is frankly cost' and 'the evaluation is gonna be so important... if it's not easy, they're not gonna use the system.' She emphasized 'advanced evaluation workflows and robust reporting are key gaps today, with emphasis on ease of bid evaluation, analytics and cost-quality balance' (12 Dec). On 9 Jan 2026 Robin confirmed 'sourcing is probably the evaluations' and 'reporting' as the top demo priorities. Jessica Lee noted on 21 Nov that they want to see 'that full source-to-pay suite' and 'how we would close that purchase order and potentially flow through to our JDE' (ERP integration is mandatory). Tanya Roach described the need for a 'unified' system that handles 'a huge portfolio' (grocery wholesale, oil and gas, insurance, agriculture, Capex, Opex) with 'flexibility' and 'standard templates' and 'correct control points' (21 Nov). Jessica confirmed on 9 Jan that the RFP scope is eProcurement-first but they want to see sourcing/contracting/supplier-management flow. Decision criteria: (1) cost competitiveness vs iValua, (2) evaluation workflow ease and depth, (3) reporting/analytics strength, (4) JDE integration, (5) unified platform breadth (source-to-pay), (6) user experience/adoption, (7) flexibility for diverse categories. All confirmed from buyer statements, no gaps.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Federated is AI Curious and open to adoption but cautious on cost and skeptical of hype. Robin McDonald stated on 9 Jan 2026 'we're definitely on board for agentic AI... we've been through a lot of conferences this year... we're getting policies in place... right now it's just about educating our team members on what AI is.' She also noted 'a lot of folks right now can kinda talk the talk but not walk the walk... we're just waiting for it to catch up and be blown away' and flagged token-based cost models as a concern. Tanya Roach and Jessica Lee did not express strong AI appetite but acknowledged user experience and efficiency are priorities, which AI can deliver. The AIS fields in Salesforce are null, so no server-computed AI appetite score exists. From the calls, Federated is progressing on AI maturity (policies, education, conference attendance) and is willing to adopt proven AI capabilities but is not leading with AI as a primary decision criterion. AI Curious tier is appropriate. Merlin should be positioned as a value-add (tail-spend savings, invoice automation ROI) rather than the lead, with cost transparency to address the token-pricing concern.

items.detail: Cost is the single biggest vulnerability. Robin McDonald stated on 12 Dec 2025 'the gap that we're seeing is frankly cost' when comparing iValua P2P options. Jessica Lee noted on 21 Nov 2025 'due to some of the costs associated with... we're doing our due diligence.' Kevin Cipollaro acknowledged on 9 Jan 2026 that switching from iValua eSourcing adds ~$500K implementation cost and offered pricing relief ('we'll find pricing alleviation') but no formal proposal was delivered. Without a quantified total-cost-of-ownership comparison showing Zycus is materially cheaper than iValua P2P expansion (license + implementation + migration), Federated has no compelling reason to switch. The ROI model and cost-competitive commercial proposal are the required deliverables to close this vulnerability.

Economic Buyer (Tanya Roach, Decision Maker) and Executive Sponsor (Kelly Thompson, AVP Finance) have not been engaged. Tanya appeared once (intro call 21 Nov 2025) but did not join discovery or demo calls. Kelly has never appeared. Robin McDonald stated on 9 Jan 2026 that 'we need to get finance team buy-in before issuing formal scope.' Jessica Lee and Robin McDonald championed the evaluation but lacked authority to drive finance approval, and the initiative was shelved on 2 Jun 2026 without escalation to Tanya or Kelly. The deal will not restart without direct engagement from Tanya and Kelly to rebuild urgency and secure budget.

The buyer paused the initiative on 2 Jun 2026 due to deprioritization and asked to 'check in next spring' (spring 2027). The RFP timeline has slipped from 'February 2026' (21 Nov 2025) to 'Q2 possibly Q3' (9 Jan 2026) to shelved. The close date was moved out to Q2 2027 (10 Jun 2027) on 2 Jun 2026, a placeholder not a buyer-confirmed date. The deal is at risk of permanent dormancy if Zycus does not successfully re-engage in spring 2027 (March-April 2027). The timeline vulnerability is that Federated has no internal urgency or compelling event driving the P2P project.

Status-quo bias and incumbent inertia are high. iValua is deployed for eSourcing, contract repository, and supplier information management, and Federated is familiar with the platform. Robin McDonald noted on 12 Dec 2025 'iValua... they have an insanely slick user interface, they're also a single code base' (positive sentiment on incumbent). The switching cost (~$500K implementation) and effort required to migrate create inertia. Jessica Lee and Robin McDonald led the vendor evaluation but did not build internal momentum to overcome the status quo, and the initiative was shelved. Without a quantified business case (ROI model) showing the cost of inaction (manual processes, lack of control, iValua cost) and executive sponsorship (Tanya/Kelly) to drive change, the status quo will win again.

Finance buy-in was never secured. Robin McDonald stated on 9 Jan 2026 'we need to get finance team buy-in before issuing formal scope.' Kelly Thompson (AVP Finance, Executive Sponsor) has never been engaged. Jessica Lee and Robin McDonald did not present a business case to finance, and the initiative was shelved on 2 Jun 2026 before the RFP was issued. Without Kelly's sponsorship and a quantified ROI model showing cost savings vs iValua, the budget for a P2P project (and the switching cost) will not be approved.

north_star_verdict.headline: Buyer shelved the initiative on 2 Jun 2026 and has been unresponsive for 167 days. No engagement since 9 Jan 2026 demo. Rep awaiting spring 2027 re-engagement window per buyer's guidance.

best_practice_check.summary: The deal is cold and awaiting buyer-initiated re-engagement in spring 2027. The highest-leverage moves are: (1) re-engage Jessica Lee in March 2027 per her 2 Jun request, (2) escalate to Tanya Roach and Kelly Thompson to rebuild urgency and secure finance buy-in, (3) deliver the ROI model showing quantified cost advantage vs iValua to overcome status-quo inertia. The competitive wedge is cost; the business case must prove it. Multi-threading to the Economic Buyer (Tanya) and Executive Sponsor (Kelly) is critical because Jessica and Robin lacked the authority to drive the project through finance approval.

competitive_position.summary: iValua is the dominant incumbent and sole live threat. Deployed since before Nov 2025 for eSourcing, contract repository, and supplier information management, iValua is the system Federated knows and the baseline for evaluation. Jessica Lee stated on 21 Nov 2025 that Federated is evaluating iValua P2P but 'due to some of the costs associated with it... we're doing our due diligence' to see if a unified platform replacement is more cost-effective. Robin McDonald confirmed on 12 Dec that iValua has 'an insanely slick user interface' and is a 'single code base' but 'the gap is frankly cost.' The decision frame is: expand iValua (P2P + keep sourcing) vs. replace with a more cost-effective unified suite (Zycus or peer). Coupa, GEP, JAGGAER, and SAP Ariba are named in Salesforce but never mentioned on calls, so their status is unknown (likely part of the Nov-Jan vendor discovery but faded or eliminated from the shortlist). The strongest current threat is iValua's incumbency and the switching cost (~$500K implementation per Kevin Cipollaro on 9 Jan 2026). The second threat is status-quo/do-nothing, which won in June 2026 when the buyer shelved the project. How we win: re-engage spring 2027, deliver the quantified ROI model showing total-cost advantage vs. iValua, demonstrate evaluation workflow and reporting superiority, and secure Tanya Roach and Kelly Thompson (Executive Sponsor) buy-in to overcome inertia.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus is well-aligned on functional requirements (unified platform, JDE integration, evaluation workflows, reporting, user experience, AI innovation) but has not yet demonstrated cost competitiveness or delivered the ROI model that would differentiate vs. iValua. Federated's criteria are: (1) cost advantage vs. iValua, (2) evaluation workflow ease and depth, (3) robust reporting/analytics, (4) JDE ERP integration, (5) unified source-to-pay platform, (6) high user adoption (ease of use), (7) flexibility for diverse categories. Zycus demonstrated all functional capabilities on the 9 Jan demo and received positive feedback ('I appreciate that you have the perspective to have that kind of strategy,' Robin McDonald re: pricing flexibility, 9 Jan). The exposed area is cost and quantified value: Zycus offered an ROI model using Federated's data (21 Nov and 12 Dec), Jessica said 'we'll probably take you up on that' (12 Dec), but it was never delivered before the deal paused. Kevin Cipollaro acknowledged the ~$500K switching cost for existing iValua eSourcing customers and offered pricing relief ('we'll find pricing alleviation to make it far more appealing,' 9 Jan), but no formal proposal was made. The fit is strong on product, weak on demonstrated cost advantage and business-case proof. The move to close the gap is to deliver the ROI model and a cost-competitive proposal when the deal re-engages in spring 2027.

value.name: Status quo / Do nothing
value.quote: The initiative has been shelved for the time being and to check in next spring as project has been deprioritized (Jessica Lee, 2 Jun 2026, per Next Step).
value.how_we_win: Re-engage Jessica in spring 2027 per her request, escalate to Tanya Roach (Decision Maker) and Kelly Thompson (Executive Sponsor) to rebuild urgency, build quantified business case (ROI model) showing cost of inaction (manual processes, lack of control, iValua cost), and secure finance buy-in to restart the RFP process.

value.detail: Cost is the single biggest vulnerability. Robin McDonald stated on 12 Dec 2025 'the gap that we're seeing is frankly cost' when comparing iValua P2P options. Jessica Lee noted on 21 Nov 2025 'due to some of the costs associated with... we're doing our due diligence.' Kevin Cipollaro acknowledged on 9 Jan 2026 that switching from iValua eSourcing adds ~$500K implementation cost and offered pricing relief ('we'll find pricing alleviation') but no formal proposal was delivered. Without a quantified total-cost-of-ownership comparison showing Zycus is materially cheaper than iValua P2P expansion (license + implementation + migration), Federated has no compelling reason to switch. The ROI model and cost-competitive commercial proposal are the required deliverables to close this vulnerability.

value.detail: The buyer paused the initiative on 2 Jun 2026 due to deprioritization and asked to 'check in next spring' (spring 2027). The RFP timeline has slipped from 'February 2026' (21 Nov 2025) to 'Q2 possibly Q3' (9 Jan 2026) to shelved. The close date was moved out to Q2 2027 (10 Jun 2027) on 2 Jun 2026, a placeholder not a buyer-confirmed date. The deal is at risk of permanent dormancy if Zycus does not successfully re-engage in spring 2027 (March-April 2027). The timeline vulnerability is that Federated has no internal urgency or compelling event driving the P2P project.
value.source: Next Step 2 Jun 2026; Avoma calls 21 Nov 2025 and 9 Jan 2026

value.detail: Status-quo bias and incumbent inertia are high. iValua is deployed for eSourcing, contract repository, and supplier information management, and Federated is familiar with the platform. Robin McDonald noted on 12 Dec 2025 'iValua... they have an insanely slick user interface, they're also a single code base' (positive sentiment on incumbent). The switching cost (~$500K implementation) and effort required to migrate create inertia. Jessica Lee and Robin McDonald led the vendor evaluation but did not build internal momentum to overcome the status quo, and the initiative was shelved. Without a quantified business case (ROI model) showing the cost of inaction (manual processes, lack of control, iValua cost) and executive sponsorship (Tanya/Kelly) to drive change, the status quo will win again.
